world.title: ‘Discontinue transit to Iran’: Trump shares US Navy video as Hormuz blockade tightens
world.summary: Former President Donald Trump shared a US Navy video on his Truth Social platform, highlighting naval operations in the Strait of Hormuz. He warned ships to "discontinue transit to Iran," reflecting the US's increasingly stringent stance on Iran amid a growing maritime blockade. This move underscores the strategic importance of the Strait of Hormuz as a vital energy corridor.

world.title: 'Blaze not yet under control': Fire breaks out at Viva Energy's Geelong refinery in Australia
world.summary: A significant fire broke out early Thursday at Viva Energy Group's Geelong refinery in southern Australia, one of the nation’s two remaining oil refineries, supplying approximately 10% of the country's fuel. Fire Rescue Victoria responded to the incident, which remains uncontrolled. The situation is ongoing, with emergency services working to manage the blaze and assess the damage.

world.title: White House denies US sought truce extension, says new talks may happen in Pakistan
world.summary: The White House stated on Wednesday that the United States has not formally requested an extension of the ceasefire with Iran. This clarification comes amid ongoing diplomatic discussions between the two nations. Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt addressed media reports, emphasizing that while talks may occur, the US has not pursued an extension of the current truce.

business.title: Not Adani, Not Ambani: This real estate giant is building India’s largest 3 GW data center park
business.summary: India's data center market is expected to grow to $22 billion by 2030, doubling from $10 billion in 2025, driven by significant investments and increasing digital demand. Major metropolitan areas are leading this capacity expansion. Property consultant Vestian reports that the sector has already attracted approximately $13-15 billion in investments, indicating strong growth potential in the coming years.

business.title: India's Apple component exports to China surge to record $2.5 billion under ECMS scheme
business.summary: India's electronics exports to China have surged to a record $2.5 billion, driven by Apple's component manufacturing scheme. Indian vendors are now exporting parts to China, marking a reversal of previous trends. This development is anticipated to significantly boost India's overall exports to China, underscoring the positive impact of government initiatives on the electronics sector.

business.title: Asia markets rise as hopes of U.S.-Iran deal boost Wall Street benchmarks
business.summary: Asia-Pacific markets opened higher on Thursday, following record gains on Wall Street. The increase in stock prices is attributed to rising expectations of a U.S.-Iran deal aimed at ending the ongoing conflict. This week has seen a rally in stocks, driven by optimism surrounding potential peace negotiations between the two nations, positively impacting investor sentiment across the region.

technology.title: India's Apple component exports to China surge to record $2.5 billion under ECMS scheme
technology.summary: India's electronics exports to China have reached a record $2.5 billion, driven by Apple's component manufacturing scheme. Indian vendors are now exporting parts to China, marking a reversal of previous trends. This surge is anticipated to significantly enhance India's overall exports to China, underscoring the positive impact of government initiatives on the country's electronics sector.

technology.title: 108 more Chrome extensions found to be injecting ads and harvesting data
technology.summary: A recent report identified 108 Chrome extensions that inject ads and harvest user data. Security experts warn that these extensions pose significant risks, urging users to delete them immediately. While many are cautious about unknown APKs, the same vigilance has not been applied to browser extensions, which can compromise privacy and security. Users are advised to review their installed extensions.

technology.title: Pragmata PC Ray Tracing vs Path Tracing performance compared
technology.summary: Capcom's Pragmata, a new sci-fi shooter, features both ray tracing and path tracing support, utilizing the RE Engine also used in Resident Evil: Requiem. This allows for a performance comparison between the two rendering techniques. The game aims to enhance visual fidelity, showcasing advancements in graphics technology within the gaming industry, particularly in real-time rendering capabilities.

sports.title: ‘I’m still not 100%’: Virat Kohli’s blunt fitness admission after RCB win
sports.summary: Virat Kohli scored 49 runs off 34 balls, leading Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a five-wicket victory against Lucknow Super Giants in IPL 2026. Despite his performance, Kohli admitted he is still not at 100% fitness. The match took place at M. Chinnaswamy Stadium, showcasing Kohli's ongoing contributions to his team's success amid personal health challenges.

sports.title: Bowlers’ day out as RCB coasts to victory against a listless LSG - The Hindu
sports.summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) won the toss and opted to bowl against Lucknow Super Giants (LSG) in IPL 2026. RCB secured their fourth victory in five matches, defeating LSG by five wickets at M. Chinnaswamy Stadium. Chasing a target of 147 runs, RCB completed the chase with nearly five overs remaining, showcasing strong bowling performance throughout the match.

sports.title: Real Madrid star blames referee for defeat against Bayern Munich
sports.summary: Real Madrid was eliminated from the Champions League by Bayern Munich, leading to frustration over officiating. Players expressed dissatisfaction with Slovenian referee Slavko Vinčić's decisions during the match. Despite some calls favoring Real Madrid, the team felt that the referee's performance contributed to their defeat. The loss has left the club and its supporters grappling with disappointment.

health.title: Heatwaves in the Indo-Gangetic Plains: Why Local Land and Atmospheric Conditions Matter More Than We Thought
health.summary: Research from IIT Bombay indicates that heatwaves in the Indo-Gangetic Plains are primarily influenced by local land and atmospheric conditions, rather than external hot air. This finding highlights the significance of regional factors in climate studies, emphasizing the urgent need to address the impacts of extreme heat on the densely populated area, which houses approximately 600 million people.

health.title: What raises vaccination rates most? Access, community outreach and incentives lead the list
health.summary: An analysis published by The BMJ identifies key factors that effectively increase vaccination rates. These include extending vaccination opportunities, engaging community members alongside healthcare professionals in vaccine communication, and offering financial incentives. The findings are based on international trial evidence, highlighting the importance of accessibility and community involvement in promoting vaccine uptake.

health.title: Hospital launches programme for high-risk heart patients
health.summary: Kauvery Hospital has launched the Complex High-Risk Indicated Procedures (CHIP) program aimed at enhancing care for high-risk heart patients. This initiative focuses on specialized and rapid treatment approaches to manage complex cardiac conditions. The program is part of the Kauvery Heart Institute's efforts to provide improved healthcare solutions for individuals facing significant heart-related health challenges.

science.title: Geologists Uncover Hidden Evidence Showing the Americas Collided Millions of Years Earlier Than Previously Believed
science.summary: Geologists have discovered new magnetic evidence from volcanic rocks indicating that the collision of the Americas began millions of years earlier than previously thought. This finding suggests that significant geological changes, including the reshaping of oceans and climates, occurred long before humans understood plate tectonics. The collision of Central and South America played a crucial role in Earth's history.

science.title: An 826-year-old Japanese poem reveals a massive solar storm that could impact future space missions
science.summary: A 13th-century Japanese poem describing a blood-red sky has been linked to a massive solar storm by researchers at the Okinawa Institute of Science and Technology. This historical account may provide insights into past solar activity, potentially aiding future space missions. The findings highlight the relevance of historical records in understanding solar events and their impact on modern technology.

science.title: Scientists Found a 'Living Fossil' Thought Extinct for 11 Million Years Thriving in Laos
science.summary: Scientists have rediscovered a rodent species in Laos, believed to have been extinct for 11 million years. This "living fossil" challenges previous assumptions about mammalian evolution and survival. The finding offers new insights into the species' resilience and adaptation in the wild, highlighting the importance of biodiversity and the potential for undiscovered wildlife in remote regions.

entertainment.title: Aquarius Horoscope Today, April 16, 2026: Mars heats your speech, and you may say things you don’t mean
entertainment.summary: On April 16, 2026, Aquarius individuals may experience heightened courage and a desire to try new things due to the Moon's influence. Mars encourages bold communication, but caution is advised to avoid saying things unintentionally. It's essential to avoid impulsive financial decisions, prioritizing peace of mind while navigating interactions with loved ones that could lead to misunderstandings.

entertainment.title: Horoscope Today: April 16, 2026
entertainment.summary: On April 16, 2026, horoscopes suggest that Aquarians may find emotional closure, recognizing patterns in their lives. This realization could lead to a sense of relief and lightness. The guidance emphasizes that matters of the heart should not be overanalyzed. The day encourages embracing newfound clarity and moving forward positively, leaving behind past cycles that no longer serve them.

entertainment.title: Asha Bhosle's death rekindles debate on end-of-life ICU care in India - Telegraph India
entertainment.summary: The death of Asha Bhosle has sparked renewed discussions in India regarding end-of-life care in intensive care units (ICUs), especially when the likelihood of significant recovery is low. Uncertainties about the treatments she received, including the use of CPR, highlight ongoing concerns among medical professionals about legal repercussions and the challenges of addressing patients' and families' wishes in critical situations.
